UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a registered user's profile information within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

defaultShippingAddressId.description: Reference to the default shipping address for the user. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Address)

defaultBillingAddressId.description: Reference to the default billing address for the user. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Address)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

newsletterOptIn.description: Indicates if the user has opted in to receive newsletters.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Represents a physical address associated with a user or an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this address belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (e.g., apartment, suite number).

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address.

isDefaultShipping.description: Indicates if this is the user's default shipping address.

isDefaultBilling.description: Indicates if this is the user's default billing address.

label.description: A user-friendly label for the address (e.g., 'Home', 'Work').

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for products (e.g., Santé, Beauté).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

slug.description: A URL-friendly version of the category name.

parentCategoryId.description: Reference to the parent category for sub-categories. (Relationship: Category 1:N Category)

Brand.title: Brand
Brand.description: Represents a brand associated with products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Brand entity.

name.description: The name of the brand (e.g., 'La Roche-Posay').

logoUrl.description: URL to the brand's logo image.

description.description: A brief description of the brand.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product available in the pharmacy's catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A short description of the product.

longDescription.description: A detailed description of the product, potentially in rich text format.

sku.description: The Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for the product variation.

price.description: The current selling price of the product.

oldPrice.description: The original price of the product before a discount, if applicable.

discountPercentage.description: The percentage discount applied to the product, if applicable.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the product.

brandId.description: Reference to the Brand of the product. (Relationship: Brand 1:N Product)

categoryId.description: Reference to the primary Category of the product. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

weight.description: The weight of the product for shipping calculations (in grams).

volume.description: The volume or quantity description of the product (e.g., '400ml', '20 ampoules').

stockQuantity.description: The current quantity of the product in stock.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the product is currently available for purchase.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the product is highlighted as a 'Best Seller' or featured item.

isPromotion.description: Indicates if the product is currently part of a promotion.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product information was last updated.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's shopping cart, holding products selected for purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this cart. Can be null for guest users. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was last updated (e.g., item added/removed).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product within a shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the Cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product added to the cart. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product in the cart.

priceAtAdded.description: The price of the product when it was added to the cart.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed purchase transaction made by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Shipped', 'Delivered').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including taxes and shipping.

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for shipping. (Relationship: Address 1:1 Order)

billingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address used for billing. (Relationship: Address 1:1 Order)

fulfillmentMethod.description: The method chosen for order fulfillment (e.g., 'Delivery', 'Click & Collect').

pickupCode.description: A unique code for 'Click & Collect' orders to be used for pickup verification.

paymentStatus.description: The status of the payment for the order (e.g., 'Paid', 'Pending', 'Failed').

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'PayPal').

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product item within a completed order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product that was ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product ordered.

priceAtPurchase.description: The price of the product at the time the order was placed.

Prescription.title: Prescription
Prescription.description: Represents a digital prescription submitted by a user for fulfillment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Prescription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who submitted the prescription. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Prescription)

imageUrl.description: URL to the uploaded image of the prescription.

submissionDate.description: Timestamp when the prescription was submitted.

status.description: The current status of the prescription (e.g., 'Submitted', 'Under Review', 'Ready for Pickup').

notes.description: Any additional notes provided by the user with the prescription.

pharmacistNotes.description: Internal notes added by the pharmacist regarding the prescription.

Author.title: Author
Author.description: Represents an author of blog posts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Author entity.

name.description: The full name of the author.

bio.description: A short biography of the author.

imageUrl.description: URL to the author's profile picture.

Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: Represents a tag for organizing blog posts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tag entity.

name.description: The name of the tag (e.g., 'Conseils Santé', 'Beauté Bio').

slug.description: A URL-friendly version of the tag name.

BlogPost.title: BlogPost
BlogPost.description: Represents an article or post published on the pharmacy's blog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlogPost entity.

title.description: The title of the blog post.

slug.description: A URL-friendly version of the blog post title.

content.description: The full content of the blog post, possibly in rich text (HTML) format.

excerpt.description: A short summary or snippet of the blog post for listings.

authorId.description: Reference to the Author of the blog post. (Relationship: Author 1:N BlogPost)

publishedDate.description: Timestamp when the blog post was first published.

updatedDate.description: Timestamp when the blog post was last updated.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image associated with the blog post.

tagIds.description: References to Tags associated with the blog post. (Relationship: Tag N:N BlogPost)

isPublished.description: Indicates if the blog post is currently published and visible.

ContactMessage.title: ContactMessage
ContactMessage.description: Represents a message submitted through the contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactMessage entity.

senderName.description: The full name of the person sending the message.

senderEmail.description: The email address of the sender.

senderPhone.description: The phone number of the sender (optional).

subject.description: The subject of the message (e.g., 'Question sur un produit', 'Suivi de commande').

messageContent.description: The actual content of the message.

submissionDate.description: Timestamp when the message was submitted.

isReplied.description: Indicates if the message has been replied to by the pharmacy.

replyNotes.description: Internal notes regarding the reply or resolution of the message.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The document ID (`userId`) must match `request.auth.uid` for ownership. This is the root for all user-owned data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores addresses associated with a specific user profile. Includes denormalized 'userId' for direct ownership verification and Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier of the address document.

definition.description: Stores digital prescriptions submitted by a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for direct ownership verification and Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who submitted this prescription.

params.name: prescriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the prescription document.

definition.description: Stores completed purchase orders made by a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for direct ownership verification and Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order document.

definition.description: Represents individual products within a user's order. Includes denormalized 'userId' (from parent Order) and 'orderId' for Authorization Independence and direct ownership validation.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the order item document.

definition.description: Stores shopping cart data. Can be associated with an authenticated user (denormalized 'userId' field) or be a guest cart. 'userId' is nullable.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shopping cart.

definition.description: Represents individual products within a shopping cart. Includes denormalized 'cartId' and 'userId' (from parent Cart, if applicable) for Authorization Independence.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent cart.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the cart item document.

definition.description: Stores product category information. Publicly readable.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the category.

definition.description: Stores brand information for products. Publicly readable.

params.name: brandId
params.description: The unique identifier of the brand.

definition.description: Stores product catalog data. Publicly readable.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores information about blog post authors. Publicly readable.

params.name: authorId
params.description: The unique identifier of the author.

definition.description: Stores tags for organizing blog posts. Publicly readable.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique identifier of the tag.

params.name: blogPostId
params.description: The unique identifier of the blog post.

definition.description: Stores messages submitted via the contact form. Publicly writable (with spam protection). Includes denormalized 'senderUid' (if sender is authenticated) for potential future sender-specific access, otherwise primarily for admin review.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the contact message.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. Authorization Independence is a critical focus, primarily achieved through strategic denormalization and consistent path-based ownership. For entities that are fundamentally owned by a user (e.g., addresses, orders, prescriptions), they are nested under a `/userProfiles/{userId}` path. This makes it straightforward to write security rules like `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`. To eliminate hierarchical authorization dependencies for subcollections, denormalization is extensively used. For instance, `userId` is denormalized into `OrderItem` and `CartItem` documents. This ensures that a security rule on an `OrderItem` document doesn't need to perform a `get()` call on its parent `Order` document to check ownership, thus enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.

Structural Segregation is applied by separating public, globally accessible data (like products, categories, blog posts) from user-specific private data. Public collections such as `/products` or `/blogPosts` are designed for broad read access, while user data is isolated in `/userProfiles/{userId}` subcollections. This clear separation simplifies security rules, as different collections have homogeneous security postures. For instance, public collections can have `allow read: if true;`, while user-specific collections have `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`.

Queryability and Authorization Postures (QAPs) are supported by ensuring that `list` operations can be secured effectively. For user-owned data, `list` queries can inherently filter by `request.auth.uid` on the `userId` in the document (or implicitly via the path, which is then validated by rules). For example, listing a user's orders would be `db.collection('userProfiles').doc(uid).collection('orders').where('userId', '==', uid)`. The denormalized `userId` within subcollection documents allows rules to validate the ownership of *every* document in a list query, preventing data leakage. For collaborative or system-level data like `contactMessages`, public write access is permitted (with assumed backend spam protection), but read access would be restricted to authenticated administrators (e.g., via a separate `roles_admin` collection, not detailed in this structure but a standard pattern for DBAC).

Cart data, which can be both for authenticated users and guests, is handled at the top level `/carts/{cartId}`. For authenticated users, `userId` is denormalized into the `Cart` document, allowing rules to secure it. This also applies to `CartItem` documents, where `userId` is denormalized from the parent `Cart` for robust authorization checks.
